title: BOOKS Evangelion Books
wikitext:
[]: [BOOKS] Evangelion Books             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 18, 2009 8:20 am I'd like to share with you a look at some of the books I've collected over the years. I enjoy collecting books. I love their presentation and tangibility. There's just something about the smell of the paper and ink, not to mention the smell of a box of stuff fresh from Japan that is just really wonderful. There are literally hundreds of Eva books out there. I have a lot of the major/official stuff, but suffice to say, when you toss in all of the variants and unofficial (read:smut) out there, this is just scratching the surface: http://www.youtube.com/view_play_list?p=2599E9E76E5C86D5 I hope you all enjoy.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Mar 18, 2009 1:31 pm oh yeah, I remember the pics of your bookcase you posted when I first started lurking many, many moons ago there should be a "Life According to EM" weekly segment or something btw, nice ponytail

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 18, 2009 1:33 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: btw, nice ponytail

[]:             Quote Post by Twsa'teotu » Wed Mar 18, 2009 5:26 pm well, there's "spinoff books" and stuff, and then theres "books about Eva"

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Wed Mar 18, 2009 6:12 pm Yeah, Eva books do have a certain nice smell. Thanks for the post. I'd assume you got these all from your mystery middleman.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 18, 2009 10:04 pm Oedipusfoot wrote: Yeah, Eva books do have a certain nice smell. Thanks for the post. I'd assume you got these all from your mystery middleman.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Thu Mar 19, 2009 4:54 am Whoa, so waaaay back in time you were all along secretely covering up...JPQueen? You're telling me you weren't using magic to get them? Wow. Wow. I thought there was some secret underground book service man. I sort of enjoyed this mystery for a while. Oh well.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 19, 2009 8:17 am Oedipusfoot wrote: Whoa, so waaaay back in time you were all along secretely covering up...JPQueen? You're telling me you weren't using magic to get them? Wow. Wow. I thought there was some secret underground book service man. I sort of enjoyed this mystery for a while. Oh well.

[]:             Quote Post by Gib » Thu Mar 19, 2009 10:28 am Evangelion Style seems great!! I only have Newtype 100%, Der Mond, ADAM,one about Girlfriend of Steel, two spanish guide (Mangazone Especial Evangelion; they are so good), the three Groundworks about series and the spanish edition of manga.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Thu Mar 19, 2009 12:30 pm I might buy Der Mond at a local book store. Your ponytail scares the shit out of me. and Nice book collection.

[]:             Quote Post by Twsa'teotu » Thu Mar 19, 2009 5:12 pm are any of these scanned? particularly the out of print ones?

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Thu Mar 19, 2009 9:28 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Didn't want to give it exposure and inadvertently make it difficult to collect certain books. Is that weird of me?

[]:             Quote Post by GVman » Sat Mar 21, 2009 10:09 pm What was the "...horrible, horrible book you should never, ever buy and never, ever read"? A guide book?

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sat Mar 21, 2009 10:29 pm Just so you know, the Australian translated release of Sadamoto's Eva Manga (published by Chuang Yi) has the full colour pages at the front of them.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Mar 22, 2009 3:16 am Yeah same with the UK version - at least for sadamoto's manga. Then again, I only ever read the first edition, so... As for the 'horrible book', he did a review of it on the evamonkey site, and it's essentialy a book which tries to explian the mysteries of eva, but fails miserably, getting consistantly confused over facts.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun Mar 22, 2009 8:00 am What ever happened to the Terminal Dogma: Essays on NGE book? There are ANF threads around here but...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 28, 2009 2:54 am Split out the Carmine tangent. Here's the part of TMBounty_Hunter's post that "didn't belong": TMBounty_Hunter Sun Mar 22, 2009 12:15 pm I'm surprised you didn't mention the huge, awesome and expensive Rebuild 1.0 Complete Collection. You don't have it? You'd probably love it. Also you mentioned some recorded panels with Japanese staff, is there any way of aquiring those? Are they youtubes or are they confined to a few CDs/DVDs around the country?

[]:             Quote Post by Riotgear » Sun Apr 05, 2009 12:10 pm I really enjoyed your videos, Eva Monkey - thankyou for posting them. I enjoyed hearing what you thought about books that I own myself, and seeing some new ones I was previously unaware of. Can you tell me why Viz's publications of the manga are quite a bit thicker than the Chuang Yi versions (which are the same size as the Japanese ones)? I saw the Viz ones at the library and I don't have access to my own manga right now to compare them - I'm assuming it's just the adverts and Carl Horn's articles at the back - is it also printed on thicker paper? I own the following books: - Evangelion Chronicle #1-30 - Evangelion Chronicle Side A - Evangelion Chronicle Side B - Evangelion Chronicle Illustrations - Der Mond - Die Sterne - Die Sterne Ver. 2.0 - Newtype 100% Collection - E-Mono - Evangelion in 3D Second Edition - Evangelion Figure Archive - Ayanami Rei Ikusei Keikaku Graphic Illustration Library - Kōtetsu no Girlfriend Visual Fan Book - Sore o Nasumono - 2015: The Last Year of Ryoji Kaji - The 3 movie booklets ('Red Cross' book etc) as seen in Eva Monkey's video - Encyclopedia Evangelions (PS2 Evangelion 2 game book) - Manga #1-11 Chuang Yi version (plus #9 Japanese special edition) - Ayanami Ikusei Keikaku with Asuka Hokan Keikaku #1-2 - Evangelion 1.0 Entry File - Evangelion 1.0 Complete Works And Carmine should reach me any day now. I have a book called 'Pilgrimage to Sacred Places' (about the real life locales seen in various anime and manga) which includes a section on Eva, a bunch of other stuff that has some Eva articles (like the Newtypes in Monkey's videos) and also something called the 'Second Impact Premium Box' - it supposedly includes a 'mini guidebook', but I've never opened it up far enough (just wanted to play with the little voice player thing that was included) so I don't know what it's like XD It's likely I've missed out something seeing as I had to go by memory here - I moved recently and almost all of my belongings are still packaged up, and will remain that way until I can afford my own house and move out of home. My favourites would be Der Mond (Sadamoto's works = love), Die Sterne (I felt so guilty buying Ver. 2.0, though), Rei Ikusei Keikaku G.I.L. (another Rei worshipper here - sorry), Sore o Nasumono (I can look at this for hours), E-Mono (they should do a new one, there's more than enough new merch to cover. Chronicle kind of does this though) and the 1.0 Complete Works - I am excited and yet utterly terrified at the prospect of a book like this regarding You Can (Not) Advance - I haven't got a new job since I moved and my savings will take a hit if they make one! I would recommend the 1.0 Entry File as a cheap alternative to the big black 1.0 bible - they'll likely make one for the other films, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 05, 2009 5:00 pm Riotgear wrote: - Evangelion Chronicle #1- 30

[]:             Quote Post by Riotgear » Mon Apr 06, 2009 2:53 am I bought each issue as they came out too, which cost me an arm and a leg because the place I bought them from (Akadot... sorry to see #30 is sold out there >.<) would ship each individual issue in an oversized box (postage cost more than the mag itself) - I ended up doing a deal with them in the end where I'd pay as each came out and they'd hold one for a bit then send them two at a time - that at least saved me on shipping. Great store though actually. The exchange rate was (is, is always and will always be) terrible between New Zealand and US dollars though... lucky I had a job that paid reasonably well. Now that I'm in Australia my dollar is stronger but I don't have a freakin' job to generate income. It's rough, noone wants to hire a filthy NZer regardless of their qualifications. Sorry, I'm going off on a tangent. Well, I do know which box most of my artbooks are in, so if I really wanted to I could open it up, find #30 and do some scans, though I don't know how well they'd come out considering I don't want to damage the magazine. The Meitantei Eva booklet was easier as it was just a few stapled pages. Okay, well, I suppose I can scan at least Yui's article, and if the text at the side of the page is unreadable (due to curving of the page... you hopefully know what I mean, I don't want to harshly bend the mag) then I'll rotate it and scan again. So it might not be pretty but it should be readable which is what matters. I have no idea where the 2I Premium Box would be, so unfortunately scans of that will have to wait. It's frustrating living in a smaller house and not being able to unpack - when I get my own place someday I can go scan crazy if you want XD Exclusive content in Side A/B/Illust? Not that I noticed in regard to articles (though I didn't do a thorough comparison), but the goods pages had extra merch that wasn't featured in the individual issues (probably not of interest to you, but I'm a merch whore so I enjoyed it). Illustrations is supposed to have 6 new images (a whopping six!) but I only noticed four new ones if I recall correctly. It's nice to have all the pictures together though, and the book is the same size as Der Mond and Die Sterne, thus it makes a good companion to them. What you likely already realise is that Side A and B do not have everything that E-Chron #1-30 have. There's far less pages in two books than in the whole 950+ pages of the individual magazines, hence the full title of the books is "The Essential Evangelion Chronicle Side A/B" - it's just what they deem most important that is present in these books. The list of your stuff that you linked to has some nice items too, some of which I would very much like to acquire myself at some stage. But first I need a job @,@
